rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSIE FLORENCE FISHER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, February 2, 2012, Line 1, RESPONDENT EDDIE STEVENSON''S DEMURRER PETITION TO REVOKE WILL is Off calendar for this department. Moving party should re-notice the motion to be heard in Department 204. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSIE FLORENCE FISHER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Demurrer Of Executor Eddie Stevenson To Petition To Revoke Will Filed Herein By Frances Jackson
rulings.Rulings: The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The petition was filed after the running of the statute of limitations. The failure to file within the statutory period is not a default that is subject to the mandatory relief portion of CCP section 473(b). In any event, Petitioner has not shown excusable neglect. Nor has Petitioner shown extrinsic fraud. Misinformation from one¿s own prior counsel cannot be charged to the other side and is not a basis for relief from the statute of limitations. Any party who wishes to argue the tentative must notify opposing counsel and the court (415-551-3702) no later than 5:00 p.m. on Wednesday, February 1, 2012.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Respondents Motion To Strike C. Mcclatchy'S Amended Petition For Declaratory Releif Pursant To Former Pc Sec. 21320
rulings.Rulings: Demurrer overruled and motion to strike denied, ten days to respond. The Court will allow the amended petition notwithstanding the one-day delay in filing. A safe-harbor petition is available for a trust that became irrevocable prior to 2001. Any party who wishes to argue the tentative must notify opposing counsel and the court (415-551-3702) no later than 5:00 p.m. on Wednesday, February 1, 2012.
